title: Melting Jamaican Pork Stew
description: A fatty and molten weeknight Jamaican dish featuring vanilla pod, slow-cooked to perfection in a Dutch oven, with macrobiotic-friendly ingredients. This stew is a hearty and comforting meal for a cold evening.
instructions: Heat the coconut oil in a Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for 1 minute.
Add the pork shoulder and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the split vanilla pod, ground allspice, and dried thyme, and stir to combine.
Pour in enough water to cover the pork and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 2 hours.
Season with sea salt to taste.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
Cook pork to an internal temperature of 63°C / 145°F and let it rest for 3 minutes before serving.
